message: Holistic Mentorship is an independent LMS domain with separate owned records while reusing canonical LMS User, Student, School, and Program identities.

Holistic Mentorship Admin is an additive feature-scoped capability; it does not replace a staff User's existing LMS role or grant non-Holistic admin powers, and global Admins inherit it automatically.

Supersedes the additive-capability decision: Holistic Mentorship Admin is a dedicated single LMS role whose feature permissions may include Holistic Mentorship and other LMS surfaces; it is not a Centre designation or an unrestricted global Admin role.
All Holistic Mentorship Admin users share one role-level feature permission set; v1 has no per-email feature-access exceptions.
In v1, the Holistic Mentorship Admin role grants only the Holistic Mentorship powers defined by the PRD; Sessions and other non-Holistic feature access are deferred.
Each Holistic Mentorship Admin has program-wide visibility across every launch school in Program 1, including school selection, Mentor-Mentee Mappings, and mentorship status.

Holistic Mentorship Admins have view-only access to the School and staff context needed across Program 1, retain the PRD's edit powers inside Holistic Mentorship, and cannot edit Students, Curriculum, Visits, or Sessions in v1.
In v1, only active Staff Management Teachers assigned to a launch School in Program 1 are eligible to be Holistic Mentors; other LMS roles and passcode users are ineligible.

Holistic Mentors retain their existing Teacher access outside Holistic Mentorship; within Holistic Mentorship they can access only their assigned Mentees and cannot view other students' or Mentors' Holistic data.

Holistic Mentorship Admins may open and read all Student Context and submitted Post-Session Notes for every Holistic Mentee in Program 1; raw source-questionnaire access remains for the privacy ticket.
Only a Holistic Mentee's currently assigned Holistic Mentor may draft and submit that Mentee's Post-Session Notes; Holistic Mentorship Admins and global Admins cannot submit on the Mentor's behalf.

Submitted Post-Session Notes are read-only until a Holistic Mentorship Admin or global Admin reopens them; only the assigned Mentor may correct and resubmit, and Admins never edit the answers directly.
Supersedes the reopen workflow: in v1, Mentors may edit Post-Session Notes only while drafting; submitted Notes cannot be edited or reopened by any actor.
Supersedes immutable submission: the currently assigned Holistic Mentor may edit their Mentee's Post-Session Notes at any time; Admins remain read-only and no reopen workflow is required.

Global Admins assign the Holistic Mentorship Admin primary LMS role through Staff Management, the forward user-management surface; LMS role remains separate from Centre designation and does not require a Centre seat.

In v1, assigned Teachers see only their Holistic Mentees, global Admins have full Holistic access, and unassigned Teachers, Program Managers, Program Admins, and passcode users have no Holistic access.

Holistic Mentorship Admins can view all eligible Students across Program 1, including unmapped Students, so they can create and manage Mentor-Mentee Mappings; assigned-Mentee-only visibility applies to Holistic Mentors, not Admins.

Supersedes Admin-managed mappings: eligible Teachers manage Mappings from the Mentorship Tab by assigning unmapped Students to themselves, reassigning another Mentor's Mentee to themselves, or removing their own assignment; each Student has at most one active Mentor, while Admins have mapping visibility only.

Holistic Mentor eligibility and Mapping roster access are scoped per launch School from active Teacher seats; a Teacher with multiple eligible seats can map Students at each School, including when they currently have no Mentees.

Refines Notes editing after reassignment: the current Mentor may read prior submitted Notes for continuity but may edit only Notes they authored; the former Mentor loses access after reassignment, and Admins remain read-only.

The v1 Holistic Mentorship launch allowlist is canonical Main DB Program ID 1 (JNV CoE) only and covers every School in that Program rather than a hard-coded School list; Poojita is the product owner for allowlist approval.

Each Program has one academic-year-specific Holistic Phase Plan containing the full ordered Grade 11 and Grade 12 Phase sequence; a new year may copy the prior Plan, while later edits do not alter historical Plans or linked records.

Each Holistic Phase has a stable internal identity and required short title; LMS derives the visible Phase number from its position in the Phase Plan rather than letting Admins enter or use the number as identity.

Phase publication is a cumulative Release: releasing a Phase makes it available for its Program, academic year, and Grade while prior released Phases stay available; the latest released Phase is Current and unreleased Phases are unavailable.

Refines Phase publication terminology: the Admin action is Open phase; the current Phase is Open and Active, earlier Phases remain Open but not Active, and future Phases are Locked. Opening the next Phase moves Active from the prior Phase without closing it.
Holistic Mentorship Admins may open Phases out of sequence and change which Open Phase is Active at any time for program needs; LMS does not enforce sequential Phase transitions.
Changing which Holistic Phase is Active changes only the current focus; every previously Open Phase remains Open and accessible.
An Admin may move an Open Phase back to Locked only before any Mentor draft or submitted Notes exist for it; after student work exists, the Phase may become inactive but must remain Open.

An Admin may permanently delete only a Locked Phase that has never had Mentor work; once opened or used, the Phase and its history are retained. V1 needs no separate per-Phase Archive action because prior academic-year Plans become read-only.

Admins may reorder or insert only among Locked future Phases with no Mentor work; once a Phase is opened or used its sequence position is fixed, while its Open and Active states can still change out of sequence.

V1 has no Admin-controlled first-Phase checkbox; LMS derives each Grade's first Phase from the earliest ordered Phase assigned to that Grade, while Student Context source and fallback rules remain a separate decision.
A Phase's Open and Active state is Program-wide for one academic year and Grade and applies to every School in that Program; v1 has no School-specific Phase state.

A Phase cannot be opened until it has a Grade, required short title, valid Phase Guidance, and at least one valid Post-Session Question; detailed content validation remains with the Guidance and Notes lifecycle tickets.
V1 uses immediate manual Open phase and later Phase-state actions with confirmation and actor/time audit; scheduled opening is out of scope.

In v1, the first persisted Post-Session Notes data for any Mentee marks the Phase as started and freezes its Grade, Phase Guidance, and Post-Session Questions; Open and Active state changes remain available to Admins.
The Phase-start freeze covers the complete Phase definition: Grade, title, sequence position, Phase Guidance, and Post-Session Questions; Admins may still change Open and Active state.

A Program, academic year, and Grade has no Active Phase before any Phase opens; afterward exactly one Open Phase is Active. Opening a Locked Phase makes it Active, and Admins may move Active to any other Open Phase.

To create a new academic-year Phase Plan, an Admin may start blank or copy the prior Plan's Grade, title, order, Guidance, and Questions into new Locked Phase records; no student work or Open/Active state is copied, and the prior Plan remains read-only.

The live 2026 Holistic Student Profile source is the Grade-specific Quiz Form and its MongoDB Sessions; current source user_id values are canonical LMS User IDs that resolve through student.user_id to student.id, while legacy data requires an explicit identifier migration.
V1 Student Profile generation may send the full questionnaire answer set to OpenRouter, but the external payload excludes Student name, User ID, Student ID, School, and other direct LMS identifiers.
V1 exposes generated Student Profile summaries rather than raw questionnaire answers in LMS; raw-answer investigation remains limited to technical access in the protected source system.

V1 prefers Zero Data Retention for OpenRouter requests but does not block processing when ZDR is unavailable; fallback still denies provider data collection and keeps OpenRouter prompt/response logging disabled.
V1 external AI processing is limited to Student Profile questionnaire answers; Post-Session Notes, Phase Guidance, Mentor-Mentee Mappings, School data, and other LMS records are excluded.
V1 Holistic storage keeps the generated Student Profile summary and provenance but does not create another copy of raw questionnaire answers already held in the Quiz and BigQuery source path.

Student Profile summaries and Post-Session Notes remain as historical records after Student exit or academic-year end; former actors lose access through normal scope rules, and verified deletion requests are handled separately.

Only a global Admin may process an AF-approved Holistic data deletion request; summary and Notes content are erased, a content-free actor/time/reason audit remains, and raw-source deletion is coordinated separately.
V1 adds no per-read audit trail for Student Profile summaries or Notes; important Holistic mutations record actor and time without copying sensitive content into audit records.

Generated Student Profile summaries cannot be manually edited; assigned Mentors may flag them, Mentorship Admins or global Admins may request regeneration, and source-answer errors are corrected at the source first.

Holistic Mappings are current-year, School-scoped self-assignments for eligible Program 1 Teachers and Grade 11/12 Students; they carry forward only while both remain eligible, retain immutable assignment history, and end automatically on eligibility loss.

V1 stores one safe Markdown Phase Guidance body authored and previewed directly in LMS; Phase state controls visibility, first Notes freeze the final content, and separate Overview, uploads, PDFs, WYSIWYG, templates, and revision browsing are deferred.

V1 uses one autosaved Post-Session Notes answer set per Mentee and Phase with 1-4 required free-text answers; first non-empty draft freezes the Phase, Submit completes it, only the current author may explicitly edit submitted content, and audits retain no prior answer text.

Holistic Mentee phase availability and progress are independent: Active is a Program highlight, missed phases stay Pending, first-year Mapping derives editable Skipped history, and Notes submission alone completes a Phase.

Holistic Student Profiles are generated once per Program 1 Student journey from the confirmed Grade 11 or Grade 12 Profile Form, using all answered Questions grouped by Question Set; an operator-run idempotent BigQuery flow stores atomic outputs by prompt version and model with retained comparison history and explicit Active configuration.

Student Context is resolved live and read-only: the entry-Grade starting Phase uses Profile or approved launch history, later Phases use the latest earlier submitted Notes across gaps and academic years, and missing context never blocks work.

Holistic and Academic Mentorship use separate School-page tabs. Teachers enter Holistic through School context and open shared deep-linkable Student Phase pages; Mentorship Admins use a Program-wide Students & Progress / Phase Setup workspace with Program and School filters.

Holistic Mentorship Admin progress shows one mapped Mentee per selected Phase, omits unmapped Students, uses current-data filtered counts and read-only drill-down, and exports filter-matched submitted Notes.
